title: Bulbophyllum sanfordii
what_description: Bulbophyllum sanfordii is a species of orchid that belongs to the family Orchidaceae. It is a monopodial epiphyte with a slender rhizome and a short, thick stem bearing a single, apical leaf. The leaf is linear, lanceolate, and dark green, measuring up to 10 cm in length. The inflorescence of B. sanfordii is a raceme with a long peduncle, bearing a few, small, greenish-yellow flowers with a short, club-shaped labellum. The plant is native to Madagascar and has been identified through the examination of botanical specimens.

The plant's antimicrobial properties have been found to inhibit the growth of various pathogens, including bacteria and fungi, due to the presence of bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and saponins.
